_comment: Per CLAUDE.md ABSOLUTE RULE #1, the policy that decides 'is this provider available' lives here as content. The Swift skeleton (AgentModelRouter.checkAvailability) just consults this file plus a fixed set of named predicates whose implementations live in ProviderAvailabilityContent.swift. To add a new flavor of 'available' (e.g. require a minimum cached-model count for Ollama), add the predicate function in Swift (one rebuild) and from then on retunes ship via this JSON.

_supportedPredicates._comment: Documentation only — the Swift registry decides what's actually callable. This list mirrors the Predicate enum in ProviderAvailabilityContent.swift so reviewers know what's allowed without diving into Swift.
_supportedPredicates.always: Always returns true. Use for providers with no enable gate (e.g. Apple Foundation Models which are always present on Apple Silicon macOS 26+).
_supportedPredicates.hasAPIKey_openai: Keychain has a non-empty OpenAI key.
_supportedPredicates.hasAPIKey_anthropic: Keychain has a non-empty Anthropic key.
_supportedPredicates.hasAPIKey_google: Keychain has a non-empty Google AI key.
_supportedPredicates.hasAPIKey_perplexity: Keychain has a non-empty Perplexity key.
_supportedPredicates.hasAPIKey_openrouter: Keychain has a non-empty OpenRouter key.
_supportedPredicates.hasOllamaModelsOrEnabled: Either OllamaEnabled UserDefault is true (legacy opt-in), OR there's at least one Ollama model cached locally (= user has installed something via Discover Models or `ollama pull`).
_supportedPredicates.hasCloudTokens: User has compute tokens via CloudTokenManager (mChatAICloud).
